planned_changes: Add a QA sheet with: rounding policy statement (display vs logic), 'Recalc OK' master check (all named ranges valid, key formulas resolve, COUNTERRORS=0), workbook version/timestamp, and Inputs short-hash.
Implement explicit per-scenario boolean columns: temp_pass, yield_pass, fatigue_pass, crack_free_pass, dimensional_rationale_pass; compute overall_pass via AND using unrounded values.

Create a Decision sheet that aggregates scenario booleans and costs, auto-selects least-cost PASS, or returns 'SCRAP/REPLACE' with an escalation note when none pass; bind Summary table to Decision outputs.
On Inputs sheet, add a provenance note and ensure every input shows units; list acceptance gates and thresholds.
Expose intermediate stress lineage on StressCalc: base_membrane_stress, kt_factor, flatness_add, computed_stress; all formulas must reference Inputs via named ranges (no hard-coding).
Add thickness-to-hole sensitivity: two subcases with conditional formatting to highlight the MIN thickness cell; automatically choose the worst-case to drive computed_stress.
Economics clarity: add an Assumptions note defining whether 'days' = downtime/lead-time; compute slip_cost = days*46,000 USD; add rank column and show cost breakdown.

Update memo: add a PASS/FAIL table aligned to workbook booleans; complete 'Required Inspections and Process Controls' with scenario-specific steps and signoffs; include an 'Expert-review items' list (mapping uncertainty, conservatisms, Kt rationale); reaffirm scrap/replace escalation since no scenario passes.
Add a small formula sanity test block (e.g., t=2.40 mm, no Kt, no flatness add => 382 MPa) and verify it prior to artifact write in the builder script.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address the ri_8 failure and strengthen weak areas (ri_2 and decision/gate transparency) to improve workbook reproducibility, traceability, and memo completeness while preserving packet fidelity.
